fields.definition_origin: Codified by Mau Baron (founder, Prayer Lock; ~$40K/mo solo founder scaling to $150K/mo by mid-2025) in his Starter Story playbook (2025) and YouTube essays ('How To Reach 10,000 App Users' 2024 and 'Why 99% of Apps Fail' 2025). Baron explicitly extended his onboarding from 5 minutes to 15 minutes and observed his organic conversion triple. The framework: affirm → social proof → demo → personalize → AI reveal → paywall, where each step EARNS the next via narrative payoff, not data extraction. Distinct from Cal-AI's data-collection-heavy 33-step questionnaire (item 17) — same family (long onboarding) but the load-bearing mechanism is identity/emotion, not metrics. Direct quote: 'as long as it's adding value and telling a story, the longer the better.'
fields.core_mechanism: Pairs Hero's Journey narrative arc (Joseph Campbell 1949) with onboarding stage-gates. Each step DELIVERS something to the user (social proof, an insight, a demo, a personalized reveal) BEFORE asking for the next thing — inverting the data-collection contract. The user feels they are being SHOWN around rather than INTERVIEWED. Mechanism stack: (a) reciprocity (Cialdini 1984) — every value-delivery primes a small commitment; (b) narrative transportation (Green & Brock 2000) — story-form processing reduces critical-evaluation; (c) identity-formation Ash 2018 — the user steps into a self-concept ('person who prays daily', 'person who runs marathons') that the AI reveal then validates; (d) sunk cost stays operative but is masked by perceived value. Compared to Cal-AI which is 'I gave you 33 data points so I should finish', Mau-Baron is 'I learned about myself across 15 minutes and now I want this version of me'.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Narrative transportation engages bilateral medial prefrontal cortex and posterior cingulate (Mar 2011 review of fiction-comprehension fMRI). Self-reference effect (medial PFC, Kelley 2002). Dopaminergic reward at each story payoff. [uncertain on direct measurement in onboarding contexts]
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Mau Baron Prayer Lock case study (Starter Story 2025, YouTube 2024-2025): 5-min onboarding → 15-min onboarding tripled organic conversion (3× lift, self-reported, single-app case). (2) Baron's $40K/mo → $150K/mo scaling within 12 months attributed in equal parts to UGC creative pipeline + story-onboarding extension. (3) Headspace 2024 onboarding (Tear Them Down teardown): identity-led structure ('Are you new to meditation?' → 'What brings you here?' → first-meditation demo → personalized recommendation → trial) hit ~70% completion vs ~40% for prior data-led version. (4) Calm 2024 onboarding similarly identity-led, attributed by founders to ARR growth past $250M. (5) Headway book-summary onboarding (insidestartups.substack 2024): demo-led vs data-led split test showed demo-led won by 18% on D7 retention. (6) Mau Baron's framework explicitly cited in 100+ practitioner threads on X, Indie Hackers, and Starter Story 2024-2025.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: End-to-end completion rate (target ≥60% for 15-min flow), trial start at paywall, organic conversion lift (Baron's headline metric — 3× for Prayer Lock), retention D1/D7/D30, narrative-completion rate (do users finish the demo?), per-section drop-off (each act of the story must hold ≥85%).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Mau Baron Prayer Lock: 3× organic conversion from 5→15 min extension (single-app case study, self-reported). Headway demo-led variant: +18% D7 retention vs data-led. Adapty 2026: long story-onboarding apps cluster at the top of the 1.35% onboarding paywall conversion benchmark.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) FSM with 5 acts: AFFIRM (welcome+pain validation, 1-2 screens), SOCIAL_PROOF (millions/testimonials, 1-2 screens), DEMO (live first-feature use, 2-3 screens), PERSONALIZE (4-6 identity questions, NOT 33), AI_REVEAL (theatrical loader + LLM-generated personalized payoff), PAYWALL. (2) Total step count: 12-20 (vs Cal-AI 33) — fewer steps, longer per-step dwell time (DEMO and REVEAL are slow). (3) Each act's exit transition narratively bridges the next: 'Now that you've seen [demo result], let's make it yours' → 'Here's the prayer/plan/playlist generated just for you' → 'Lock it in with [paywall]'. (4) Identity questions (item 27) emphasized over demographic — 'Are you a runner / walker / beginner?' beats 'What's your age?'. (5) Social-proof punctuation interleaved between acts ('5M people pray daily on Prayer Lock'). (6) AI reveal endpoint generates not just a plan but a STORY about the user's transformation. (7) Cross-step stitching (item 14) at every reveal moment. (8) Paywall framed as 'lock in your new path', not 'subscribe'. (9) UGC creative-onboarding alignment (item 42) — first 3 screens mirror the TikTok/Spark Ad the user clicked.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Prayer Lock (Mau Baron) ~15 min: welcome+pain → 5M users social proof → DEMO of first prayer → 'Who are you praying for?' → 'What's your biggest spiritual struggle?' → identity ('I am a person who prays daily') → loader → AI-generated personalized prayer reveal stitched with their inputs → paywall framed as 'lock in your daily practice'. (2) Headspace 2024 ~10 min: welcome+pain ('What brings you here? Anxiety / Sleep / Focus') → demo (60-sec breathing exercise live) → 'When do you want to meditate?' → identity → personalized plan reveal → trial. (3) Calm 2024 ~12 min: welcome → soundscape demo → 'What's your sleep struggle?' → identity → personalized sleep plan reveal → trial. (4) Headway ~8 min: welcome → 'What do you want to learn?' → demo (15-min summary preview of one book) → reading-goal commitment → personalized reading plan → paywall. (5) Blinkist (insidestartups teardown) similar identity-led arc: welcome → goal → demo → personalized plan → trial. (6) Mojo (video-creation app, Mau Baron alumni network) explicit story-onboarding: 'Make your first reel' demo INSIDE onboarding before any paywall ask.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary. Mobile's full-screen attention + thumb-zone gesture + haptic supports the immersive narrative arc. On web, equivalent flows lose to multi-tab abandonment. Web equivalent: typeform-style narrative + embedded video demo + paywall checkout — Headway and Blinkist run web variants with measurably lower conversion than mobile.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) translucent material + bolder left-aligned typography is purpose-built for narrative-led onboarding — each act feels like its own scene. M3E (Android 16+) expressive shape + motion system supports the act transitions and themed reveal screens. Both DLs handle the demo-as-onboarding sub-flow (item 23 interactive walkthrough) natively.
fields.category_fit: Strongest fit: wellness/habit (Headspace, Calm, Prayer Lock), AI-wrapper consumer (story-led suits identity branding), edtech (Headway, Blinkist), journaling (Reflectly), fitness (Strava community-led variant), spiritual / faith / meditation (Hallow, Pray.com, Prayer Lock), creative tools (Mojo). Acceptable: dating apps that lead with identity (Hinge prompts arc). EXCLUSION: utilities (overkill — use cinematic 4-screen item 25), B2B (Linear/Notion are minimal-onboarding by doctrine), fintech (regulatory + trust posture clashes with story-arc), pure data-collection categories (Cal AI's metric-first approach beats story for hard quant outcomes like calorie tracking).
fields.tooling_options: Plotline (visual flow + variants), Storyly (story-format onboarding native), Adapty Pro (paywall orchestration), Superwall (paywall + onboarding A/B), Lottie / Rive (rich animation for the demo act), OpenAI/Anthropic for AI reveal endpoint, Mau Baron's tooling stack (per Starter Story interview): React Native + Adapty + Supabase + OpenAI + TikTok Spark Ads pipeline.
fields.ai_dependency: required: the AI reveal at the climax is what converts identity investment into peak emotion. Mau Baron's Prayer Lock AI-generated personalized prayer is the load-bearing payoff. Use a frontier-class model for reveal because output is open-ended and quality-sensitive (GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet) — small models produce generic output that breaks the story.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude Design to model the FSM as 5 narrative acts with explicit story-bridge transitions. Prompt fragment: 'Build a story-onboarding FSM in XState v5 with 5 acts: affirm → social_proof → demo → personalize → reveal → paywall. Total ~15 screens. Each act exit fires a `narrative_bridge` action that sets the next screen's headline using the prior act's payoff token (`Now that you saw [demo result], let''s make it yours`). The PERSONALIZE act has 4-6 IDENTITY questions (not demographic) — "Are you a [runner/walker/beginner]" not "What''s your age range?". The REVEAL screen runs a 5-7s theatrical loader, then displays an LLM-generated personalized [prayer/plan/playlist] that stitches ≥3 user inputs. The PAYWALL is framed as "Lock in your [transformation]". Forbid: 33-step Cal-AI extraction; demographic-heavy openings; reveal that doesn''t reference the user''s identity word; paywall framed as "subscribe".'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a 5-act story-onboarding flow in 12 screens: ACT 1 AFFIRM (1) welcome with brand mark + pain headline ("Most people pray inconsistently. We''re changing that.") (2) reassurance. ACT 2 SOCIAL PROOF (3) avatar grid "Join 5M who pray daily". ACT 3 DEMO (4) live first-prayer experience (audio play button + transcript). ACT 4 PERSONALIZE (5) "Who are you praying for?" 4 identity options (6) "What''s your biggest struggle?" 5 chip options (7) "How often do you want to pray?" frequency wheel (8) commitment confirm. ACT 5 REVEAL (9) loader "Generating your personal prayer..." with cycling copy (10) reveal — full-screen prayer card stitched with user''s inputs as bold tokens. (11) review prompt overlay. (12) paywall framed "Lock in your daily practice" with side-by-side annual/monthly. Use cinematic full-bleed photography for ACT 1-3, conversational chip-style chrome for ACT 4, ceremonial gold-accent reveal for ACT 5.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically (1) flattens story acts into homogeneous question screens (loses the narrative bridges), (2) skips the demo act entirely (which is the load-bearing 'value before ask' move), (3) uses demographic questions in PERSONALIZE ("What's your age?") instead of identity questions ("Who are you praying for?"), (4) generates a generic reveal that doesn't stitch user inputs, (5) frames the paywall as 'subscribe' instead of 'lock in your transformation'. Counter-prompt: 'Output the FSM with 5 NAMED acts (affirm, social_proof, demo, personalize, reveal, paywall). The demo act MUST contain a live working preview of the core feature (not just a video). The personalize act uses IDENTITY questions only — forbid age range, gender as demographic. The reveal MUST stitch ≥3 user inputs verbatim. The paywall headline must NOT contain the word "subscribe".'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Story-as-decoration with no value delivered per act (just longer Cal-AI extraction). (2) Skipping the demo act ('we'll show you in-app') — defeats the reciprocity mechanism. (3) Demographic-heavy personalize act (kills identity formation). (4) Generic AI reveal (kills the climax). (5) Paywall mismatch — 'subscribe to continue' breaks the lock-in-your-path framing. (6) Toggle paywall (banned 2026-01). (7) Acting cute when category is serious (Jester voice in mental-health onboarding). (8) UGC-creative misalignment (item 42) — first 3 screens don't mirror the ad creative.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. Same risks as long-questionnaire (item 17): GDPR Article 9 special-category if health/spiritual/biometric data; FTC Click-to-Cancel symmetry; EU Digital Fairness Act dark-pattern audit. Story-form has SLIGHT advantage under DFA because reciprocity-based flows (value before ask) are read as less manipulative than pure sunk-cost extraction. But identity-formation pressure can be flagged if it crosses into 'addictive design' (e.g., 'You're now a Daily Pray-er — break the streak and lose your identity').
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Same as item 17. Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization is friendlier here (story uses fewer questions). Apple Guideline 3.1.2 toggle-paywall ban (2026-01) — paywall in story flow must use side-by-side plans. Apple Guideline 4.8 sign-in alternatives if the story includes account creation. Android Play Data Safety form must declare data types.
fields.accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 — narrative arc helps screen-reader users (clear act boundaries are signposting). Demo act must be alternate-input accessible — if demo is a swipe gesture, provide a button. Audio demos (Calm, Prayer Lock) must have transcripts. Reveal screen typography under Liquid Glass / M3E must scale with dynamic-type. prefers-reduced-motion replaces theatrical loader with static text. Avoid identity-formation pressure that excludes users ("You are a runner" excludes wheelchair users — broaden to "You are someone who moves").
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Reveal quality is everything — story builds anticipation; flat reveal collapses it. (2) Demo act requires real product code (not a video) — adds dev cost. (3) Latency at reveal — 5-7s loader is the buffer; LLM call must finish by then. (4) Cost — frontier model per reveal at scale ($0.01-$0.05 per user); cache by similar input profiles. (5) Localization — narrative bridges and identity language don't machine-translate; hire native copywriters per locale. (6) UGC-creative pipeline (item 42) is part of the doctrine — story onboarding without ad-aligned welcome screen leaks conversion at the source.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Strong fit globally with localization. Mau Baron Prayer Lock localized to 8 locales with native-copywritten narratives — translation alone insufficient. JP/KR: story-form resonates (manga / drama tradition); reduce direct social-proof bragging. MENA: Prayer Lock has high MENA traction; faith-led narrative is culturally aligned. LATAM: story-form + aggressive paywall discount anchoring (Adapty 2026). CN: shorter 8-min variant outperforms 15-min in Sensor Tower 2024 data for super-app context.
fields.prescribed_action: Use the Mau Baron 5-act story-onboarding (12-20 screens, 5-15 min) for identity-led categories (wellness, faith, meditation, edtech, fitness, journaling). Architect: AFFIRM → SOCIAL_PROOF → DEMO (live, working) → PERSONALIZE (4-6 identity questions, never demographic) → AI_REVEAL (theatrical loader + LLM stitched payoff) → PAYWALL (framed as 'lock in your [transformation]', side-by-side plans). Each act delivers value before asking for the next thing.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: PERSONALIZE act uses identity questions (item 27) only, sequenced from low-commitment to high-identity (item 26). Compounds with cross-step stitching (item 14) at the reveal.
fields.aha_relevance: The DEMO act IS an early Aha moment (live first-feature use builds time-to-value < 60s). The AI_REVEAL act is the engineered peak Aha. Story onboarding produces TWO Aha moments — demo Aha + reveal Aha — which compounds activation.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Magician (transformation arc, AI reveal as the spell), Hero (the user's journey), Caregiver (gentle pacing, reassurance), Sage (wisdom-led demo). Strong mismatch with Outlaw (rebellion archetype reads narrative-arc as conformist). Acceptable with Lover, Innocent, Explorer.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Frictionless / Deferred Pattern (item 19) — explicit opposite. Pairs against extraction-heavy Cal-AI long questionnaire (item 17) when category demands identity over metrics.
sources: [How To Reach 10,000 App Users (Beginner Marketing) — Mau Baron YouTube](https://youtu.be/e_t1gYkvZkI)
[Why 99% of Apps Fail — Mau Baron YouTube](https://youtu.be/aYzZ6VywNa0)
